metadata.description: Giesselmann-Schmidt-Catran DD estimator with WB tertiary enrollment, panel Granger falsification, and reverse-causality tests for SDET hypothesis.

Scaling UNDP MYS \u00d7 10 reduces collinearity in DD products."}, "bias_summary": {"undp_mys_bias_dd_minus_naive": 155.21265021660994, "ter_enroll_bias_dd_minus_naive": 13.126087352509566, "interpretation": "Positive bias: naive FE-product UNDERSTATES the triple interaction effect. DD estimator corrects for within-group confounding of products."}, "coverage_report": {"n_total": 161, "n_ter_observed": 137, "n_ter_observed_2015_19": 80, "n_ter_observed_2020_22": 57, "n_education_imputed_with_ter": 57, "within_sd_ter": 3.668453361475643, "within_sd_undp": 0.026051782242125735}, "granger_limitation": "ILO SocProt annual data is too sparse for Granger tests (10 democratizer countries with \u22655 observations). SAP\u2192SocProt path_b serves as the reverse-causality falsification for SocProt."}
